**1st and 2nd Periodic reports**  
**Management internship starting in Sept’24**

*Complete the dedicated sections in this document. For this, please use the **Tab function** from your keyboard to jump in the document from one section to the other and your **Mouse** to select the appropriate box for drop-down list.*

**Objectives:**  
During your internship, you will need to submit 2 periodic reports in Onstage. It is important for your HMSM internship supervisor to be aware of your activities, understand your successes and challenges in order to coach you and be able to assess your internship performance at the end of the period. Additionally, in these periodic reports you are asked to write about some topics that will be part of your final report. Finally, each periodic report is also the moment to explain your progress with regards to your Business Process Improvement project. Your internship supervisor will provide you with valuable feedback and challenge you with his/her feedback. 

**Instructions:**  
You will need to complete this form as per the instructions below for each paragraph. These periodic reports, when well written, save you time when writing your Final report and/or Business Process improvement report. You will quickly notice that the periodic reports are built in a way to support you to successfully complete the final products you hand in towards the end of your internship. You must therefore study the *General Assessment Form* and the *Business Process Improvement Process Student Instruction* on Moodle (Execution phase). These documents clarify the content on which your reporting will be assessed. 

Each periodic report must be uploaded in OnStage in the corresponding activity. Each periodic report must be validated and approved by your HMSM internship supervisor. In case your periodic report is evaluated as insufficient by your internship school supervisor, you will be required to submit a new version of this periodic report.

**Deadlines\*:**

- Week 6: 1st Periodic report   
- Week 13: 2nd Periodic report

*\*Note: these weeks are calculated from the starting week of your internship. Refer to your Internship timeline to review the specific dates you and your supervisor agreed on.* 

| Name of student | : | Thomas Wielnga |

| Internship company name | : | Aedes |
| Internship department | : | Hospitality |

| Title company mentor | : | Head of Hospitality |
| Telephone no. company mentor | : | \+31 (0) 6 52 03 83 93 |
| E-mail address company mentor | : | joost@aedes.co |

**Paragraph 1 – Internship content and learning achievements**  
**Overall, in this paragraph we ask you to explain (what, how, when, where) but more importantly to REFLECT (why).** 

*1st Periodic report:*   
*1\. Reflect on your tasks and responsibilities.*   
*2\. Reflect on your contribution to the company via the achievement of these tasks.* 

*2nd periodic report:*  
*1\. Reflect on your tasks, responsibilities and how you are making progress in terms of responsibilities.*   
*2\. In the reflection, focus on how you are contributing to the Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) of your department/company via these tasks.*

After my first 6 weeks at Aedes, the projects I have worked on thus far range from strategic planning to operational support. Most of my activities, from a hospitality point of view, relate to the Program of Requirements (PoR) for Amstel 111\. For this I need to plan and document in detail the experience of guests that the hospitality department wants to achieve with this work. This ranges from room layout to more practical things like waste management. Anything and everything, in the end, adds to giving the guests a seamless, wonderful experience. I've been working quite closely with Joost, our head of hospitality—and also my boss/mentor here—understanding his vision of the property and whether it meets the Aedes standard of guest comfort and operational efficiency. Ongoing, one of my responsibilities entails assisting Joost in meetings, writing down the action points, following up on suppliers, and getting quotes. These may sound administrative; however I found, they are crucial in keeping the project on the road. Every meeting brings along new challenges or adjustments in our plan, and the timely organization of action points makes sure that there is no delay in the execution of major decisions.

I have also been involved in finance-related activities, like assisting with the month-end closing activities, booking invoices, and stock level monitoring. Though those are not hospitality related jobs perse, the experience has provided me with the ability to understand how financial management interlinks with all facets of hotel operations, from procurement right through to timely completion of projects.

Generally speaking, I participate in the Amstel 111 critical path research within a broader context of my BPI research question on improving Aedes operational efficiency. The project addresses how insourcing at every level of hotel exploitation-from development and project management through operations to asset management-can minimize delays and other risks and maximize productivity and gains. As an analyst in this study, I can learn about any operational inefficiencies that arise and devise ways in which processes can be streamlined. Reflective Thoughts on My Responsibilities and Tasks

The responsibilities that I have been given, particularly those that relate to the PoRs, have helped me understand in greater depth how guests feel from a hospitality point of view and what Aedes is trying to achieve with their new hotel.

Everything matters-from even small elements, such as where the light switch is located to how a waste management system is put in place-I found all can impact the bottom line of the hotel and guest satisfaction. This has put my attention into both practicality and aesthetics. Working with Joost has given me a lot of insight into high-level decision-making. His role is always a balancing act among the different perspectives: development, operations, and guest experience, and this influences how I approach my work to make sure to consider all stakeholders in designing the PoRs for instance. I also need to perform more administrative tasks such as the transcription of meetings and follow-up with suppliers. This has taught me how to communicate and provide structure in the management of projects. It is a valuable experience in managing any project involving coordination across departments and vendors with competing agendas.

**Reflection on My Contribution to the Company**

My involvement in financial activities like month-end closing gave me an understanding of how financial health may relate to or affect project management. This really put me in a better position to understand hotel operations and financial discipline in accomplishing a project. 

Through these, I have been able to contribute tangibly to Aedes in many instances, most importantly, in streamlining the PoRs for Amstel 111\. I feel that by aligning it with both operational needs and a refined guest experience, I have added long-term value to the project thus far, helping ensure consistent guest comfort and relaxed luxury across the project.

The additional support of Joost gave a boost to the efficiency of meetings and decision making of Delhaize by increasing the pace of gathering action points, following up on suppliers-the alternative being delays at each turn, and smooth cooperation between teams and suppliers not being guaranteed, a possible bottleneck in the development process.

Complementing the long-term Aedes strategy, my critical path analysis work on Amstel 111 explores how insourcing influences risk and productivity to help build the foundation for more effective initiatives in the years to come. This could result in the management of better projects with a lesser reliance on third-party contractors, which are typically associated with faster schedules and lower costs.

Lastly, my inputs to the finance team are month-end closing, booking of invoices, and stock monitoring that have helped in keeping financial processes for the Aedes projects right on target to maintain financial stability and resource allocation.

**Paragraph 2 – Preparation for Final Report**  
**In this paragraph, you need to report on different topics depending on the periodic report you need to upload.**  
**Please make sure you also list the sources used, using APA referencing. Check this [aid](https://apastyle.apa.org/instructional-aids/reference-examples.pdf) or check the one on [Library](https://libguides.bibliotheek.zuyd.nl/processing/introduction)\!**

*1st periodic report:*   
*Company analysis on the basis of a Business Model Canvas and explaining whether disalignments are found between building blocks will suffice at this point (Final Report: Chapter 1\)*

*2nd periodic report:*   
*Leadership styles analysis (Final Report: Chapter 3\)*

![][image1]

**Analysis of Aedes’ BMC**

**Customer Segments**  
Aedes targets very wide customer sectors, including but not limited to hotels, individuals (tenants), and businesses (also renters of real estate). These represent both B2B and B2C tactics.

**Value Proposition**  
Aedes' value proposition—"Creating places with heart and soul that have a positive impact"—shows that they want to be sustainable and to develop responsibly. There is, however, a slight mismatch in how this value proposition is realized in various client segments. For example, hotel operations and real estate asset management have different measures of success; guest satisfaction would be the measure of success for the former, while appreciation of assets would be for the latter. In those cases, Aedes' mission does not explain how such "positive impact" would come into play. One possibility I see is to reword the value offer so that accrued benefits of each segment are featured. Hotels would, therefore, likely to focus on guest experience, and real estate might focus more on long-term financial and social sustainability. 

**Channels**  
Aedes does a multi-channeling using social media, networking, and conventions. Through this, it is easy to target B2B as well as B2C markets.

**Customer Relationships**  
Aedes maintains relationships through landlord services, automated services, and communities.

**Revenue Streams**  
Aedes creates value from a variety of revenue streams-speculation in the purchase and sale of real estate, real estate development, renting, asset management, and hotel operations. This diversification is at the same time its strength and its weakness. The complexity of the hospitality portfolio and that of real estate may set up conflicting demands for resources. Aedes could tone down this inconsistency by making it a specialized company within itself-one dealing with real estate development and another dealing with hospitality management since this is the case at present. It would allow the firm to align its resources and capabilities more worthily with each revenue stream's demands. 

**Key Resources**  
Aedes' key resources are skilled people, financial resources, physical assets and intellectual property, among others.

**Key Activities**  
Its main activities include feasibility studies, research, design, financial control, hotel management amongst others.

**Important Allies**  
Aedes depends on real estate agents, contractors, architects and consultants as key partners.

**Cost Structures**  
The cost structure for Aedes includes operational costs, cost of labor, and project-based expenses together. One area of incongruity in this regard is that the operations cost may turn out to be in the higher side since the company covers a wide range of activities. This can be reduced by streamlining the processes and use of technology-driven efficiencies, such as even more than currently the case, automated financial management system. A lean management style, especially regarding asset management and operation would enable Aedes to be profitable at the same time achieving operation excellence.

**Paragraph 3 – Preparations for Final report**  
**In this paragraph, you need to report on different topics depending on the periodic report you need to upload.**  
**Please make sure you also list the sources used, using APA referencing. Check this [aid](https://apastyle.apa.org/instructional-aids/reference-examples.pdf) or check the one on [Library](https://libguides.bibliotheek.zuyd.nl/processing/introduction)\!** 

*1st Periodic report:*   
*Contribution to two targets related to UN Sustainable Development Goals (Final Report: Chapter 4\)*

*2nd Periodic report:*   
*Departmental analysis using the balanced scorecard model using relevant performance indicators (Final Report: Chapter 2, paragraph 1\)*

Aedes is fully dedicated to connecting its projects with the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), I have observed that they focus particularly on Goal 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities and Goal 12: Responsible Consumption and Production. They actively contribute to these goals and are devoted to moving them forward through new initiatives and sustainable practices.

**Goal 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities**

**Creating Places with Lasting Relevance**

Aedes' view on sustainability is to "enhance urban environments by integrating eco-friendly design and construction practices" (Aedes, 2024a). This is in line with SDG 11, which aims to make cities and human settlements inclusive, safe, resilient, and sustainable (United Nations, 2024).

**Key Achievements and Innovations over the Years at Aedes with Their Projects:**

- 2011: The Aquifer Thermal Energy Storage system introduced in Burgerziekenhuis represented their concerns about energy consumption and sustainable building. (Aedes, 2024a)  
- 2012: The Sedum roofs and the ATES in Andaz Amsterdam brought amenity to energy efficiency and biodiversity-enabling sustainable urbanism. Aedes 2024a.  
- 2017: Solar panels and landscape restoration work at the Landgoed Vels estate symbolize the high standards of sustainability, in that resilience in urban areas is promoted.  
- 2018: An example of water resource management with the green-blue roof and Permavoid system that has already been installed in Soho House Amsterdam makes it more sustainable in respect to its urban infrastructure.  
- 2020: The zero-gas apartment building in Sarphatistraat 24 symbolizes their interest in carbon footprint reduction and energy-efficient residential construction, toward mitigation of urban pollution levels.  
- 2021: The Mannoury smart roof project brings forth insight into sustainable roofing. This furthered their knowledge on implementing science to build resilient urban areas.

**Innovation Laboratory**

Aedes also has its innovation lab, Aedes Cares, which researches new concepts relating to sustainability. In this regard, it has support for glass recycling and advanced solar panels (Aedes, 2024a). These, therefore, add to SDG 11, as they reflect innovative responses towards sustainable urban living.

**Sustainability PoR**

Aedes' sustainability approach integrates cutting-edge principles of energy performance and operation practices in all projects. This holistic approach provides for and surpasses the minimum requirements of sustainability, therefore directly contributing to SDG 11 by ensuring that projects are sustainable and resilient.

This can be noticed, for example, in Amstel 111-a project currently under construction right beside Carre. Here, I contributed by upgrading the sustainability PoR of Aedes which will be used for all future hotel developments.

**Amstel 111 Project**

- Core: Focus on reusing existing materials contributes to SDG11 by following sustainable construction methods respectful to the historical value of the building.  
- Extra: Amenities like native plants planting and insect hotel will add to more urban biodiversity, making the ecosystem of the urban resilient.  
- Innovation: Upcycling of materials and electric boat transport proves our concern for innovative solutions to sustainably support urban development.

In the final analysis, Aedes is committed to Goal 11 of the Sustainable Development Goals—that is, Sustainable Cities and Communities—through its innovative environmental-friendly initiatives. Aedes promotes sustainable urbanism and resilience in cities by the integration of systems such as ATES and Sedum roofs, pushing the envelope for such techniques as green-blue roofs and smart roofing. Further, their innovation lab and sustainability PoR showcase how they work on building long-lasting and environmentally sustainable urban settings. Really bringing home their commitment to this SDG and sustainability in general.

**Goal 12: Responsible Consumption and Production**

**Quality Sustainability**

Their commitment to sustainability is not confined to a single SDG. For example, they can act on SDG 12 by facilitating the efficient use of resources and waste reduction across all functions of the business. United Nations 2024\.

Aedes has developed a set of criteria for Core (the minimum), Extra (over and above), and Innovative (out of the ordinary) sustainability measures for its residences. Some examples of these are as follows:

**Core Measures**

* By using low-emission, FSC-certified material and designing demountable building elements, Aedes intends to minimize waste and ensure responsible material usage.  
* Air quality and material specification for a better living space and efficient use of resources. 

**Additional Measures** 

* Assuring a room is optimized for quality sleep is their pledge to sustainable consumption with better overall health and comfort. This is further evidenced by the fact that one of the USPs of Aedes is the promise of "best sleep ever.".  
* Including blue roofs and high-quality materials use fosters environmental sustainability; in that respect, it enables responsible production and consumption.

**Innovative Measures**

* Upcycling of glass for windows, application of biobased materials reduces general waste, promotes the use of sustainable materials for SDG 12\.  
* Zero-waste application and making sure of radical transparency serve as our reflections to efficient resource use and responsible means of production.

To sum up, Aedes' innovative measures set the company apart as an industry leader in setting new norms on sustainability. These are aligned perfectly with SDG 12 and contribute to better operational efficiency and environmental stewardship, indicative of Aedes's commitment toward a more sustainable future.

**Future Contributions**

If I were to continue with Aedes in a working/professional capacity, I would look forward to enhancing Aedes' contribution to SDG goals by:

1. More sophisticated measures are developed and applied to capture sustainability aspects, such as reduction of carbon footprint and resources efficiency. Such novel KPIs might be of relevance and increase the transparency of their environmental footprint.  
2. Institutionalize and promote new practices that are beyond what existing standards set in place to ensure continuous improvement and carry out evolving sustainability goals. As reviewed by Cleveland and Morris (2014) and Gibson (2006), although this is already being done by Ester—it feels like an area that one can always refine and improve.

However, I need to add that since Aedes is a very innovative company in terms of sustainability, this contribution to the continuous research for further improvement in reducing its ecological footprint might be quite hard to top.

**References:**

Aedes. (2024a, January). *Aedes places to stay*. Retrieved September 13, 2024, from [https://www.aedes.co/en/our-places/koningin-wilhelminaplein-880/](https://www.aedes.co/en/our-places/koningin-wilhelminaplein-880/)

Team Hospitality, Aedes Places. (2024b, August 23). *PoR 33\. \- Sustainability*. Unpublished manuscript.

United Nations. (2024). *Department of Economic and Social Affairs*. Retrieved September 13, 2024, from https://sdgs.un.org

Cleveland, C. J., & Morris, J. (2014). *Measuring sustainability: A practical guide*. Routledge.

Gibson, R. B. (2006). *Sustainability assessment: Criteria and processes*. Routledge.

**Paragraph 4 – Your Business Process Improvement (BPI) report**  
**In this paragraph, you need to explain your progress with regards to the BPI project.**  
**Please make sure you also list the sources used, using APA referencing. Check this [aid](https://apastyle.apa.org/instructional-aids/reference-examples.pdf) or check the one on [Library](https://libguides.bibliotheek.zuyd.nl/processing/introduction)\!** 

*1st Periodic report:*   
*Step 1, 2 and 3 of the business process (identify, discover and analyse) as clarified in figure 2 of the student instruction (see Moodle) and your plan of approach for steps 4 to 6*

*2nd Periodic report:*   
*Step 4 and 5 of the business process at least (process redesign and implementation) and 6 (process monitoring and controlling) in great detail as clarified in figure 2 of the student instruction (see Moodle)*

*Midterm visit or call:*  
*Make sure to update your supervisor and get further guidance*

*Note: Feel free to send your business improvement project update in a separate document if this is more convenient to you and upload it via OnStage in the same periodic report activity. Otherwise, include the BPI project update in the box below.* 

My BPI project aims at studying stakeholder engagement and coordination in the insourcing of hotel exploitation, using the Amstel 111 project as a case study. I want to understand how risk-delay can be minimized and productivity gains maximized within the different phases of the projects. Currently, I am working on steps 1 to 3 of the BPI process: Process Identification, Process Discovery, and Process Analysis.

**Step 1: Identify the Process**

Stakeholder involvement and integration across various stages of hotel exploitation were the processes identified to be improved. The various stages of hotel exploitation consist of the following (Piliouris, 2020):

1. Feasibility Study, Business Case, Concept  
2. Design & Licensing  
3. Selection of Contractor(s)  
4. Construction and Quality Control  
5. Project Delivery, Soft/Pre-opening, Closure

All of these are an integrated part of delivering a hotel project successfully in time, such as Amstel 111\.

Primary stakeholders would include Amber and Rowin from development, Jeroen and Wesley from project management, Madelon from asset management, and Ajar, Joost, and Wesley from operations. I focused on the need to improve timely stakeholder involvement at each stage to facilitate ease of communication without falling behind in the schedule. This was quite clear after having discussions with Joost, as sometimes communications between stakeholders were not given a timeline, thereby resulting in lost communication at times during the performance of the work.

***Why This Process?***

Engagement of stakeholders at the right time is very vital for the smooth running of such a complex project. Based on the research so far, delays are often observed when the stakeholders are not aligned as to what they are supposed to do, or due to breakdowns in communication across departments. My chosen process has this very interaction at its center, where it ensures the reduction of avoidable delays that cost in terms of time and resource utilization. Besides, improvement in the same will return immediate and quantitative results, hence becoming an area of utmost priority for optimization. 

**Step 2: Process Discovery**

The objectives of the discovery phase are to state precisely when and how stakeholders need to be involved, what their roles are, and where main communication breakdowns or bottlenecks take place. It involves scheduling interviews with key stakeholders such as Amber, Rowin, Jeroen, Wesley, Madelon, Ajar, and Joost in order to research their experiences concerning the current process.

With these interviews, I have begun to outline the process flow. Some guiding focus questions in my interviews include: 

* At what part of the process should each stakeholder be most active?  
* What are the major breakdowns in communication or inefficiencies in communication?  
* How are responsibilities divided, and where is accountability unclear?

Interviews conducted so far reveal that not clearly defined feedback loops between stages are definitely one of the sources of confusion. For example, there are bottlenecks where jobs overlap between departments, resulting in inefficient resource (time) allocation.

The process that I will be mapping out will show the beginning and end points of each step, decision points, and the schedule for stakeholder involvement. 

**Step 3: Process Analysis**

From here, I will analyze information obtained from the process map and interviews. My analysis will focus on understanding where the inefficiency in the tasks or phases was immense and where the delays were more notorious. I will also be looking if it is relevant to apply some frameworks such as COSO and RI\&E in assessing the risks around communication breakdowns, project delays, and quality control issues.

Key Questions:

* Where are delays most frequent?  
* What steps are redundant or add little value?  
* Which stages are contributing most to bottlenecks or miscommunication?  
* How do decision-making processes affect project timelines?

From my preliminary analysis/interviews, communication delays between project management and operations are the largest bottlenecks at least in my view during the Construction & Quality Control phase. There is also some lack of clarity into accountability; some tasks fall between teams, which therefore wastes time or resources. From my own observation I would say that such delays in communication and accountability issues are significant contributors to the overall project delay(s).

**Planning for Steps 4 to 6**

In the Process Redesign phase, I would suggest strategies that would solve these gaps and bottlenecks in communication. Some of the strategies I will consider include the following:

* Clearly make lines of communication between the departments, most especially in the transition into successive phases, for example, from design to construction.  
* Phase in feedback loops through the PDCA method to ensure that all stakeholders have timelines and clear tasks to make sure the next phase cannot continue without their completion.  
* Enhanced accountability so it should be very clear what is expected of each stakeholder at every stage of the process, so no task falls between the cracks.  
* Decisions reached at such key meetings are better documented and communicated with absolute clarity to all relevant stakeholders.

The following set of hypothetical measurable KPIs will guide these redesign strategies:

* Time saved during decision-making phases  
* Reduced project delays driven by improved communication  
* Overall increased responsiveness by stakeholders to action points.

I also plan to create a plan with the stakeholders at my Process Implementation stage so that they test these changes in a particular phase, say Construction & Quality Control, before setting them forth in all phases. Testing of new communication protocols with accountability measures will thus be imperative so that they function as intended and do not bring into existence new inefficiencies.

Lastly, in the Process Monitoring and Controlling stage, I will be providing a framework that Aedes can apply so that they will be able to go back regularly to the meetings with stakeholders and verify whether the redesigned process is still successful. I plan to establish performance reviews, taking into account KPIs defined earlier, in order to ensure improvements, result in actual increased efficiencies of projects.

**References:**

Piliouris, G. (2020, February 26). The 5 stages of hotel project management for a new accommodation \- Hotelier Academy. *Hotelier Academy \- Useful Information & Tips for Hoteliers*. https://www.hotelieracademy.org/the-5-stages-of-hotel-project-management-for-a-new-accommodation/

**Paragraph 5 –Progress on your learning and development goals**

*For this paragraph, please explain your progress and actions towards reaching the learning and development goals you have set down in your Personal Development Plan (PDP). Remember to use the STARR method to describe and prove your progress in a systematic and complete way (**S**ituation, **T**ask, **A**ction, **R**esult, **R**eflection).*

| *1\.* | My goal is: "to understand and effectively implement the pre-opening process of a hotel from an operations perspective, focusing on the integration and management of the property within its real estate context". However, due to higher-priority queries, I've had limited opportunities to actively work on this goal so far. My role is to contribute to Amstel 111's pre-opening process by identifying important operational strategies and how they match with real estate objectives. Although I have not had the chance to focus on the pre-opening process, I have begun working on the Critical Path (analysis) for Amstel 111\. This project has provided me with significant insight into the many phases and how different stakeholders in operations and real estate work together to guarantee a smooth pre-opening process. Working on the Critical Path has helped me gain a better grasp of the complexity of harmonizing operations and real estate planning. This will prepare me to go further into the pre-opening process in the coming weeks when the workload is expected to lighten. The delay in working on this goal has taught me that sometimes other tasks take priority—things beyond your own control—and you must accept that.  |
| ----- | :---- |
| ***2\.*** | I want to learn more about the development of a sustainable water-based delivery system for Amstel 111 in Amsterdam, focusing on the reasoning behind this choice. My objective is to investigate the reasoning for opting for this strategy and obtain insight into its possible benefits and drawbacks, with an emphasis on long-term sustainability and logistics efficiency. I have had talks with Madelon and Joost, both of whom are vital to this process. These early interactions helped me recognize that decreasing the burden on Amsterdam's streets is a key reason. This system, however, will not be feasible till 2030, since water-based travel currently is a less developed form of delivery. Nevertheless, it is expected to save money over time, with its initial feasibility and full implementation being around about 2030\. I now understand in a deeper context why this mode of transportation is being looked into, as well as the long-term benefits it could provide. Both from the sustainability and cost perspective. However, I will need to have additional talks to get an even better grasp of both its benefits and drawbacks. The process, so far, has taught me the value of engaging stakeholders early on and asking the proper questions to gain a full understanding of complex projects. I intend to go deeper into the financial and logistical ramifications in future interactions to improve my understanding.  |
| ***3\.*** | My goal is to improve the quality and effect of my research reports, specifically by creating high-quality PoRs that AEDES could apply to future projects. This will help me realize the major objective, which is enhancing my analytical skills. So far, considering the tasks that I highlighted earlier, namely, review of existing PoRs, their analysis regarding their state, identification of areas of improvement, writing new PoRs based on these observations, getting comments about my writing, and tracking improvement over time. I have done the following:  Written approximately 20 PoRs so far, including key areas like room design, waste management, and lighting systems. Completed Action 1: review of the existing PoRs for strengths and weaknesses. Completed Action 2: assured that after identifying areas for improvement, further work will be considered necessary to make each PoR meet operational and design needs. Started with Action 3: writing the PoRs and seeking feedback on them. One is done, meaning all departments have signed off on it, hence achieving the set standards on all levels. Through this process, I have enhanced my capability to make comprehensive and quality PoRs. The first completed PoR is (I think) a big achievement; now, I have a very good platform for further improvements in report writing and analysis. This process showed me how important this step-by-step approach is. Being able to accomplish the first two steps provided me with the insight needed to make relevant contributions. |
| ***4\.*** | My goal is to become a better listener, improving my ability to understand and respond to others without interrupting or jumping to conclusions. This, in my opinion, is imperative in building better relationships, making better decisions, and enhancing my leadership potential. To do this I came to the conclusion that I had to employ active listening practices, ask for feedback from coworkers, and implement the method of pause-think-respond in response to further improvement on understanding others. Actions I have taken: During the meetings, I began summarizing others' inputs to be sure I got their point across before responding. I may refer to the discussion that went on between Joost and Madelon where, instead of trying to find a direct solution, I sought clarification. I make it a habit to always try to pause briefly before answering in conversations, since this allows me to process what has been said much better and respond thoughtfully. This approach is particularly helpful during the discussion of projects, where decisions may be complex and involve many different stakeholders. I also asked colleagues/higher-ups, such as Joost, his opinions about me after certain meetings. As a result, I’ve noticed significant improvement in my communication. I have so far received positive feedback on my listening skills during assessments. This has also led to more productive conversations and better decision-making during team projects. Implementing these techniques has greatly enhanced my ability to engage with others, making me a more effective communicator. By actively listening and pausing before responding, I’ve built stronger relationships and improved collaboration within the team. I’m confident that continuing to refine these skills will further contribute to my leadership development. |
| ***5\.*** | Click or tap here to enter text – Maximum 1000 words. |

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
